sources_audited.note: The May 2023 OEWS page supports the broad NAICS 811100 occupation and wage mix, including cost estimators, office support, customer service, bookkeeping, automotive body repairers, and vehicle cleaners. It does not isolate NAICS 811121 or directly produce the packet's wage-weighted AI-exposure value; the packet discloses that transformation and population mismatch as a PROXY.
BLS Table 2.11 reports NAICS 811120 employment of 297.5 thousand in 2024 and 305.1 thousand in 2034, a 2.6 percent increase.

The cited data.census.gov profile loads only an application shell in the fetched content; neither the NAICS 811121 definition nor the claimed 35,029 employer establishments appears, so the registered claims cannot be verified from this citation.

The BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook projects 2 percent employment growth for automotive body and glass repairers from 2024 to 2034 and states that collision-prevention systems may reduce accidents while cameras and sensors add repair work.

FTC guidance supports flat-rate and actual-time labor pricing, diagnostic charges, written estimates, parts disclosure, and approval for additional work. It does not measure AI implementation or insurer bargaining and benefit pass-through, which the packet appropriately treats as ESTIMATE and explicitly caveats.

The SBA fact sheet reports that 22 percent of employer-business owners purchased their businesses and that 64.5 percent of owners planned to sell. These are all-industry ownership-path and intention statistics, not five-year completed qualifying control transfers for body shops; the PROXY bridge and error direction disclose that limitation.
FHWA's Spring 2025 baseline forecasts light-duty VMT growth averaging 0.6 percent annually from 2023 through 2043, matching the registered demand-driver claim.

The IIHS bibliography page reports statistically significant reductions in rear-end striking crash involvement for FCW and AEB systems, including 27, 43, and 50 percent reductions across the studied configurations.

caveats: S3 does not expose the cited Census definition or 35,029-establishment figure in fetched page content. This is an unsupported registered statistic, but it is decorative to the already-ESTIMATE eligibility primitive and does not change the LOW_PRIORITY base tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.

The a=0.21 value is a model-authored wage-weighted task transformation of broad NAICS 811100 occupational data rather than a reproducible value in S1. Its PROXY label, bridge, wide range, and ancestor-industry caveat are honest.

S5 documents consumer repair pricing and authorization practices but supplies no direct evidence for rho=0.58 or q=0.50, particularly insurer-platform implementation and five-year pass-through. Both primitives remain transparently ESTIMATE and keep the action model-conditioned.

S6's purchased-business incidence and sale intention are different constructs and populations from five-year completed qualifying transfers. The s5 bridge acknowledges this, but the base remains weakly anchored.

The d5 range translates ten- and twenty-year employment, VMT, and rear-end-crash evidence into five-year constant-quality service demand; the direction is supported, but the exact 1.05 base is judgment and employment is not service quantity. The packet discloses these limitations.

S8 studies selected 2010-2014 passenger-vehicle models and rear-end crashes, so its reductions cannot be generalized to all collision damage or the full five-year demand basket; this limitation is stated in the packet.
summary: Mechanics pass and the LOW_PRIORITY conclusion follows from a modest, transparently proxied AI-exposed labor layer against a predominantly physical repair process. The lens is coherent, primitive semantics are not reversed or double counted, and estimate-heavy inputs are labeled honestly with meaningful ranges and change evidence. The record is useful with caveats: S3's registered Census claim is not visible in fetched content, and the exact a, rho, q, s5, and d5 bases rely on disclosed transformations or judgment rather than direct measurements. None of those weaknesses, corrected or downgraded to its honest state, changes the base tier or the MODEL_CONDITIONED / VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.
